name: Skolt Sami

ancestors.notes: Descent per Glottolog classification of Skolt Saami (skol1241).

notes: Skeleton. Skolt Sami is an Eastern Saami language of the Uralic family, written since 1973 in a Latin orthography rich in special letters. The map covers the base Latin letters and the language-specific graphemes: â (near-open central vowel), õ (close-mid central vowel), ä and å, the caron letters č š ž, the barred/hooked consonants đ (ð), ǥ (ɣ) and ǩ (palatalized k), and ŋ. The orthography also marks suprasegmental palatalization with a prime (ˊ/ʹ) and encodes consonant-gradation quantity distinctions; these palatalization marks and length contrasts are not captured by this single-symbol map and are left unencoded, raising the PER floor.

sources.author: Hammarström, H. et al. (Glottolog 5.0)
sources.title: Glottolog: Skolt Saami (skol1241)
sources.publisher: Max Planck Institute for Evolutionary Anthropology
